message:
chore(beads): close session-harness issues, update tracker timestamps

- Close flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5 (streaming/webhook/session fixture

- Close flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5.4 (host_e2e integration + debug guide)

- Unblock flywheel_connectors-o9lja.10 (verification pass) to in_progress
- Add work-started comment on flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14.2 (live-suite layer)

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","title":"Provider-sandbox and nightly live-suite program for connectors that cannot stop at local fixtures","description":"Background:
- Some connectors cannot be fully proven by local fixtures alone; they need vendor sandboxes, bridge daemons, or nightly live runs with controlled credentials and cost caps.
- Without a formal live-suite policy, teams either skip live checks entirely or run fragile ad hoc scripts that produce poor diagnostics.

Deliverables:
- Define and implement the provider-sandbox/nightly-live layer for connectors that need it.
- Establish secret management, account provisioning, cost ceilings, cleanup policies, scheduling, flaky-test quarantine, and artifact retention.
- Record which connectors must have live suites, which can rely on local fixtures, and which require both.

Acceptance criteria:
- Live verification is part of the official testing story rather than tribal knowledge.
- Failures from nightly/provider suites produce the same structured artifacts and redaction guarantees as local E2E runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:38.290317Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.970913Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","live","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:38.290317Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:47.980156Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.229073Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.477071Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.721570Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:48.970319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.14.1","title":"Classify the connectors that require live or nightly suites and document why","description":"Background:
- Some connectors cannot truthfully stop at local fixtures because the real boundary is a managed SaaS sandbox, a device lab, or a provider behavior that cannot be emulated safely.
- The repo needs an explicit classification of those exceptions instead of ad hoc tribal knowledge.

Deliverables:
- Classify which connectors require `live` or nightly suites, record why local_non_mock is insufficient, and map the minimum acceptable live boundary.
- Feed the result back into the connector-family sweeps and CI expectations.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.15","title":"CI gates, coverage dashboards, and maintainer playbooks for the testing-completeness program","description":"Background:
- A testing program is not real until CI and maintainer workflows enforce it.
- The workspace needs both a quality gate and a practical operator story for running, triaging, and extending the new suites.

Deliverables:
- Add CI/reporting gates that fail when connectors lack required suite classification, when E2E artifacts do not validate, or when redaction/logging policies are violated.
- Generate machine-readable and human-readable dashboards for coverage status by connector family and archetype.
- Publish maintainer/operator playbooks describing how to run local acceptance suites, how nightly live suites behave, how to inspect artifacts, and how to classify or quarantine flakes.
- Ensure new connector work cannot silently ship with only fake-backed tests unless explicitly exempted under the policy.

Acceptance criteria:
- Coverage policy and artifact policy are enforceable by automation.
- A future agent can pick up any connector and know exactly which suites to run and how to debug  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.6","title":"Build reusable DB, storage, queue, browser, and local-process fixture harnesses","description":"Background:
- Database, storage, queue, browser, bridge-backed, and local-process connectors cannot all be validated through one HTTP fixture story.
- Many of these surfaces require seeded local services, temp directories, subprocess orchestration, or platform-specific helper layers.

Deliverables:
- Build reusable harnesses for DB/storage/queue/local-process categories: seeded PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite/Redis-style fixtures, object/blob fixtures, local CLI/process wrappers, browser harness integration, and bridge-backed/mobile-desktop helper flows where the connector truthfully depends on a sidecar or daemon.
- Standardize startup contracts, health probes, cleanup, seeded data, and artifact capture.
- Document where truly platform-specific coverage must live (for example macOS-only connectors) versus what can run in generic CI.

Acceptance criteria:
- Connector families that depend on databases, local filesystems, subprocesses, or bridge daemons have a credible non-mock acceptance path.
